title: aldama angustissima
intro: Aldama angustissima, also known as the Mexican tea or narrow-leaved Aldama, is a plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. The roots and leaves of the plant contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which are responsible for its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves or roots in hot water, and it can also be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
